name: Orgonite
description: Orgonite is a substance claimed by its proponents to transmute negative energy into positive energy. It typically consists of a combination of metal shavings, resin, and quartz crystals. The concept is rooted in the theories of Wilhelm Reich, who coined the term 'orgone' to describe a universal life force energy. Reich believed that orgone energy could be harnessed and used for healing and other purposes. Orgonite proponents claim that it can neutralize electromagnetic fields (EMF), chemtrails, and other forms of environmental pollution, as well as improve physical and emotional health. They often cite anecdotal evidence, personal experiences, and before-and-after photographs to support their claims. Mainstream science largely dismisses orgonite as pseudoscience. Reich's theories have been widely discredited, and there is no scientific evidence to support the claims made about orgonite's ability to transmute energy or have any measurable effects on EMF, chemtrails, or human health. The purported effects are often attributed to placebo effects, confirmation bias, or misinterpretation of data. Skeptics point to the lack of controlled studies and the reliance on anecdotal evidence as reasons to reject orgonite's purported benefits. Furthermore, the claimed mechanisms of action are inconsistent with established scientific principles of physics and chemistry. Despite the lack of scientific support, orgonite remains popular in New Age and alternative health communities.
subtitle: Purported energy transmutation device.

claims.claim: Orgonite transforms negative energy into positive energy.
claims.evidence: Anecdotal reports of improved mood, sleep, and overall well-being in proximity to orgonite; alleged neutralization of negative environmental energies.
claims.criticism: Lack of scientific evidence; subjective experiences are not reliable indicators; no measurable energy transformation has been demonstrated; placebo effect is a more likely explanation.

claims.claim: Orgonite neutralizes electromagnetic fields (EMF) and chemtrails.
claims.evidence: Some proponents claim EMF meters show reduced readings near orgonite; visual observations of chemtrail dissipation after deploying orgonite.
claims.criticism: EMF readings are often misinterpreted or manipulated; chemtrails are a conspiracy theory with no scientific basis; meteorological phenomena are misidentified as chemtrails; no controlled studies demonstrate EMF neutralization.

claims.claim: Orgonite improves physical and emotional health.
claims.evidence: Personal testimonials and anecdotal accounts of pain relief, reduced stress, and improved immune function.
claims.criticism: Lack of controlled clinical trials; subjective reports are unreliable; placebo effect is a powerful influence on perceived health benefits; no biological mechanism to explain how orgonite could affect health.

keyFigures.name: Karl Welz
keyFigures.role: Popularized the use of resin matrices with metal and crystals.

keyFigures.name: James DeMeo
keyFigures.role: Continued Reich's research and promoted orgone accumulators.

keyFigures.name: Steven Novella
keyFigures.role: Neurologist and prominent skeptic who has critiqued orgone theory.

mythologyConnections: Possible connections to ancient concepts of vital energy or life force, such as Prana or Chi.

timeline.event: Wilhelm Reich develops and promotes orgone theory.
Orgonite becomes popular in New Age and alternative health communities.

sources.author: Novella, Steven
sources.work: A Skeptical Look at Orgone Energy
sources.title: Novella, Steven
